article_title: Corrective actions and duty of information
obligation_text: Providers of high-risk AI systems which consider or have reason to consider that a high-risk AI system that they have placed on the market or put into service is not in conformity with this Regulation shall immediately take the necessary corrective actions to bring that system into conformity, to withdraw it or to recall it, as appropriate.
scoring_text: Skill in AI system non-conformity response, including conformity monitoring, corrective action decision-making, system withdrawal and recall procedures, and regulatory notification processes.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in AI system non-conformity response, including conformity monitoring, corrective action decision-making, system withdrawal and recall procedures, and regulatory notification processes.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI conformity assessments under the EU AI Act, including risk classification determination, technical documentation evaluation, and compliance assessment against applicable high-risk AI system requirements.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 20(1) (obligation) superset of WIDAI RC domain. RC-S-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.503, cosine: 0.717). Secondary: AG-K-008 (AG, cosine: 0.636), AG-S-007 (AG, cosine: 0.626).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.0/10 (raw 0.503). Shared vocabulary: [ai, conformity, skill, system]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [action, corrective, decision, making, monitoring, non] vs WIDAI terms [act, against, applicable, assessment, assessments, classification] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.891 (P=0.892, R=0.890). Balanced P/R — the 21-word competency implication and 27-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.2%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.717 > STS 0.503 (gap +0.214). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, conformity, skill, system] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 18.4%. Shared tokens: [ai, conformity, skill, system]. EU AI Act-only: [action, corrective, decision, making, monitoring, non]. WIDAI-only: [act, against, applicable, assessment, assessments, classification].

signals.detail: STS 0.503 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.214 with low STS 0.503. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
